Notice Of Motion To Stay; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; And Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Oct-23-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MARY LAU's Motion To Stay.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to stay this litigation as to her is granted. Defendant killed plaintiffs' family members in a March 2024 collision at a bus stop; she faces felony charges in a criminal case and tort allegations in this case. The impact on defendant's self-incrimination rights from civil discovery and trial is obvious. (Evid. Code 940; Pacers, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1984) 162 Cal.App.3d 686, 688-89.) Plaintiffs say they "have a compelling interest in prosecuting this action to justice before other witnesses' memories fade further and documents are gone." (Opp. 2:2-4.) But the possibility of documents disappearing in our electronic age is remote, and plaintiffs may move to lift all or part of the stay if the criminal case stalls. Barring that, the stay shall remain until the criminal case ends.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
